title: Mutable fields should not be public static
description: Mutable fields should not be "public static"Analyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellcweunpredictableWhy is this an issue?More Infopublic staticmutable fields of classes which are accessed directly should be protected to the degree possible. This can be done by reducing the accessibility of the field or by changing the return type to an immutable type.This rule raises issues forpublic staticfields with a type inheriting/implementingSystem.ArrayorSystem.Collections.Generic.ICollection<T>.Noncompliant code examplepublic class A { public static string[] strings1 = {"first","second"}; // Noncompliant public static List<String> strings3 = new List<String>(); // Noncompliant }Compliant solutionpublic class A { protected static string[] strings1 = {"first","second"}; prot
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

never_trigger_on: Properties with => syntax returning readonly types
logic.reasoning: Detects public static fields with mutable collection types (List, Dictionary, Array, IList, ICollection, etc.) that are not marked as readonly. Targets Expression nodes where C# field declarations appear, PropertyDeclaration nodes for properties, and ClassDeclaration nodes for context. Uses regex patterns to identify public static modifiers followed by mutable collection types, while excluding readonly fields, const fields, primitive arrays, IReadOnly collections, and properties that return readonly versions. The rule improves security and maintainability by preventing external modification of static collection fields.

should_trigger: public static int[] Values = new int[10];
should_not_trigger: public static IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> Settings { get; }
